Utility functions for processing ANCS notification and application attribute data in BLE communication.

# Purpose
The code is a C source file that provides utility functions for handling Apple Notification Center Service (ANCS) attributes. It includes functions to verify the completeness of notification and application attribute responses, as well as to extract attribute pointers from a data buffer. The file includes several header files, such as `ancs_util.h`, `ancs_types.h`, and others related to logging and system utilities, indicating that it is part of a larger system that interacts with ANCS over Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE).

The main functions in the file are [`ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response`](<#ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response>), [`ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict`](<#ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict>), and [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>). These functions check if the received data contains all required attributes and extract pointers to these attributes if they are present. The functions use assertions and logging to handle errors and provide feedback on the processing of ANCS data. The code defines a specific color for logging (`FILE_LOG_COLOR`) and uses macros and data structures to manage attribute lists and their properties. This file is likely intended to be part of a library or module that processes ANCS data in a system that supports BLE communication.

### ancs\_util\_is\_complete\_notif\_attr\_response<!-- {{#callable:ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.c#L34>)

Checks if a notification attribute response is complete based on the provided data and length.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to the notification attribute data buffer.
    - ``length``: The length of the data buffer.
    - ``out_error``: A pointer to a boolean that will be set to indicate if an error occurred.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assert that `out_error` is not null using `PBL_ASSERTN` macro.
    - Calculate `header_len` as the size of `GetNotificationAttributesMsg`.
    - Check if `length` is greater than `header_len`.
    - If true, initialize an array `attributes` to store pointers to `ANCSAttribute`.
    - Call [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>) with adjusted data and length to check for completeness and store the result in `complete`.
    - Return the value of `complete`.
    - If `length` is not greater than `header_len`, return `false`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the notification attribute response is complete, otherwise `false`. Sets `*out_error` to indicate if an error occurred.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>)

### ancs\_util\_is\_complete\_app\_attr\_dict<!-- {{#callable:ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.c#L49>)

Checks if the application attribute dictionary is complete by verifying the presence of all required attributes after the App ID.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to the buffer containing the application attribute data.
    - ``length``: The length of the data buffer.
    - ``out_error``: A pointer to a boolean that will be set to indicate if an error occurred during processing.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assert that `out_error` is not null using `PBL_ASSERTN` macro.
    - Iterate through the `data` buffer to find the end of the App ID, which is marked by a null character.
    - If the end of the App ID is not found and `length` becomes zero, set `*out_error` to false and return false.
    - If the end of the App ID is found, call [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>) to check if all required attributes are present in the remaining data.
    - Return the result of [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>).
- **Output**: Returns a boolean indicating whether the application attribute dictionary is complete. If an error occurs, `*out_error` is set to true.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>)

### ancs\_util\_get\_attr\_ptrs<!-- {{#callable:ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.c#L69>)

Extracts and validates ANCS attributes from a data buffer, matching them against a list of expected attributes.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: Pointer to the buffer containing ANCS attributes.
    - ``length``: Size of the data buffer in bytes.
    - ``attr_list``: Array of `FetchedAttribute` structures representing the expected attributes.
    - ``num_attrs``: Number of expected attributes in `attr_list`.
    - ``out_attr_ptrs``: Array of pointers to `ANCSAttribute` structures where matched attributes will be stored.
    - ``out_error``: Pointer to a boolean that will be set to true if an error occurs.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assert that `out_error` is not null and initialize it to false.
    - Check if the data length is smaller than the size of `ANCSAttribute`; if so, log an error, set `out_error` to true, and return false.
    - Initialize an array `attrs_found` to track which attributes have been found.
    - Iterate over the data buffer, extracting `ANCSAttribute` structures and checking if each attribute matches an entry in `attr_list`.
    - If an attribute is found, check if its length is valid; if not, log an error, set `out_error` to true, and return false.
    - If `out_attr_ptrs` is not null, store the pointer to the matched attribute in `out_attr_ptrs`.
    - If an attribute is not found in `attr_list`, log an error, set `out_error` to true, and return false.
    - After iterating, check if all non-optional attributes in `attr_list` have been found; if not, return false.
    - Return true if all attributes are extracted completely and correctly.
- **Output**: Returns true if all required attributes are found and valid, otherwise returns false. Sets `out_error` to true if an error occurs.
